item.title: The Foundation of Attachment in Emotionally Focused Therapy (EFT)
item.description: Exploring how attachment theory serves as the map for the EFT therapist.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Exploring how attachment theory serves as the map for the EFT therapist.

item.title: Bridging the Gap: Integrating EFT and the Gottman Method
item.description: How to combine two of the most popular models of couple therapy.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: How to combine two of the most popular models of couple therapy.

item.title: Mapping the Dance: De-escalating Negative Interaction Patterns
item.description: Understanding the cycle of conflict and how to break it.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Understanding the cycle of conflict and how to break it.

item.title: The Body’s Role in Bond: EFT, Polyvagal Theory, and Neurobiology
item.description: The neurobiological basis of relationship distress and repair.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: The neurobiological basis of relationship distress and repair.

item.title: The EFT Tango: Mastering the Moves of Emotional Change
item.description: The core process of restructuring interactions in EFT.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: The core process of restructuring interactions in EFT.

item.title: Softening Shame (Blamer Softening)
item.description: Transforming critical demands into vulnerable reaching by addressing the shame core.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Transforming critical demands into vulnerable reaching by addressing the shame core.

item.title: EFT and Sexual Intimacy
item.description: Integrating attachment safety with erotic desire: mapping the sexual attachment cycle.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Integrating attachment safety with erotic desire: mapping the sexual attachment cycle.

item.title: Integrating IFS and EFT
item.description: Intimacy from the Inside Out: helping partners take responsibility for their internal systems.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Intimacy from the Inside Out: helping partners take responsibility for their internal systems.

item.title: The Architecture of Attunement
item.description: Mastering Task 2 micro-skills: Reflection and Validation.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Mastering Task 2 micro-skills: Reflection and Validation.

item.title: Beyond the Surface: Evocative Responding
item.description: Using imagery and tentative reflections to expand emotional experience.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Using imagery and tentative reflections to expand emotional experience.

item.title: Turning Up the Heat: Heightening
item.description: Systematically intensifying specific responses to bring core emotion to the fore.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Systematically intensifying specific responses to bring core emotion to the fore.

item.title: The Empathic Bridge: Conjecture
item.description: Inferring and seeding attachment longings and fears to move past impasses.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Inferring and seeding attachment longings and fears to move past impasses.

item.title: Indirect Paths: Disquisitions
item.description: Using narrative and self-disclosure to bypass resistance in avoidant couples.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Using narrative and self-disclosure to bypass resistance in avoidant couples.

item.title: Mapping the Dance: Tracking the Cycle
item.description: Externalizing the interactional pattern as the "common enemy."
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Externalizing the interactional pattern as the "common enemy."

item.title: Shifting the Frame: Attachment Reframing
item.description: Shifting from character defects to attachment-based vulnerabilities.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Shifting from character defects to attachment-based vulnerabilities.

item.title: Mastering the EFT Enactment
item.description: Turning intrapsychic experience into interpersonal bonding events.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Turning intrapsychic experience into interpersonal bonding events.

item.title: The Withdrawer’s Re-engagement
item.description: Helping the withdrawn partner move from silence to active assertion.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Helping the withdrawn partner move from silence to active assertion.

item.title: The Blamer’s Softening
item.description: Moving from critical protest to the direct expression of attachment needs.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Moving from critical protest to the direct expression of attachment needs.

item.title: EFFT for Blended Families and Step-Parenting
item.description: Navigating loyalty binds and competing attachments in stepfamily systems.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Navigating loyalty binds and competing attachments in stepfamily systems.

item.title: EFT for Chronic Illness in Couples
item.description: Transforming illness from an attachment threat into a shared "team" challenge.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Transforming illness from an attachment threat into a shared "team" challenge.

item.title: Neuro-Affirming EFT for ADHD and Autism
item.description: Translating between different neurological operating systems to foster secure bonds.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Translating between different neurological operating systems to foster secure bonds.

item.title: Inclusive EFT for Queer and Trans Couples
item.description: Using attachment as a safe haven against minority stress and external oppression.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Using attachment as a safe haven against minority stress and external oppression.

item.title: The EFT Therapist's Self-Care
item.description: Managing "Self-of-Therapist" loops to stay regulated in high-conflict sessions.
item.itunes:author: Therapy Talks
item.itunes:summary: Managing "Self-of-Therapist" loops to stay regulated in high-conflict sessions.
